previous.because: ServiceNow's worst-ever single-day -18% on Apr 23, IBM same-day decline, and chained Salesforce / Workday / Oracle falls expose the displacement axis.
previous.given: Capital markets now weigh AI displacement of legacy workflow SaaS against AI cost savings, repricing seat-based revenue.
previous.so_that: Gartner / Forrester make Agent-native vs Legacy SaaS explicit and market-cap math moves from seats x ARPU to share of agent-touched API calls.
previous.landing: Inside Q2 2026; another 3-5 names likely take 15%+ drops over two quarters.
previous.plain_language: ServiceNow's stock had its worst day ever because investors think AI agents will replace its software. Other workflow SaaS firms are next.

updated.because: Apr 23 ServiceNow -18% with chained Salesforce/Workday/Oracle falls is now historical fact.
updated.given: Capital markets now underwrite agent-touched API share as the workflow-SaaS valuation metric.
updated.so_that: Follow-on names (Workday/Atlassian/Box/Smartsheet) take 15%+ drops as Gartner/Forrester codify the Agent-native axis.
updated.landing: Q2 2026 close; trigger landed May 5, watching 3-5 follow-on -15% prints.
updated.plain_language: The ServiceNow shock now counts as history; next, watch whether 3-5 more workflow SaaS names take 15%+ drops by quarter end.

rationale: Prediction landed this week (huge_longshot_hit_at=2026-05-05) with so_that VR observed_relevance=4 confirming the metric reframing crystallized; the original because is now a recorded event, so reasoning is reframed forward to the next-phase precondition (follow-on -15% prints completing the cohort reset by Q2 end).
